description: Overview of a device health status. It includes resources, interfaces and BGP information if available.

panels.description: Source-of-truth metadata for `$device`, pulled live from Infrahub via the Infinity datasource. The Maintenance flag is toggled with `nobs autocon5 maintenance` and feeds the alert rules' inhibition logic.
panels.title: Device Details

panels.description: Up/down rollup of the device's reachability, derived from sonda's `ping_response` metric (~10s cadence). UP flips red if no successful ping has landed in the last minute.
panels.title: State

panels.description: Time elapsed since the device's last reboot, from `device_uptime`. Resets to 0 when sonda restarts the scenario — uncommon, but worth knowing if you see an unexpected dip.
panels.title: Uptime

panels.description: Rolling memory utilisation across the device's memory banks. Synthetic but shaped like a real router — slow growth with occasional GC dips.
panels.title: Avg Mem

panels.description: Rolling CPU utilisation across the device's cores. The shape comes from sonda's sine-wave generator — realistic 'breathing' rather than flat data.
panels.title: Avg CPU

panels.description: Round-trip time from the synthetic prober. Healthy values are sub-millisecond on the lab network; spikes mean the scenario is modelling a degraded link or the host is loaded.
panels.title: Latency

panels.description: CPU and memory plotted together so you can correlate workload bursts with memory growth. Both come from sonda's `cpu_used` and `mem_used` series.
panels.title: CPU & Mem Usage (Avg)

panels.description: Sum of all interface throughput on the device, both directions. A quick "is this device busy?" check before drilling into per-interface panels.
panels.title: Bandwidth Usage

panels.description: Which pipeline currently delivers metrics for this device. srl1 is shipped directly by sonda (`pipeline=direct`); srl2 routes through Telegraf for normalization (`pipeline=telegraf`). Same metric schema, different paths — that's the workshop's hybrid story.
panels.title: Metrics Pipeline

panels.description: Which pipeline currently delivers logs for this device. srl1 logs are pushed directly by sonda (`pipeline=direct`); srl2 syslog is shipped via Vector (`pipeline=vector`). Number shown is log lines received in the last 5 minutes.
panels.title: Logs Pipeline

panels.description: Per-interface operational state over the dashboard's time range. Red bands are interface-down events; `nobs autocon5 flap-interface` flaps show up here within ~10s.
panels.title: Interfaces State

panels.description: Count of interfaces currently in operational state DOWN. Zero in steady state — a non-zero value usually means a flap exercise is in progress.
panels.title: Down

panels.description: Per-interface roll-up: admin state, operational state, IP, and the intended state pulled from Infrahub. Sorted alphabetically so srl1 and srl2 line up consistently for the demo.
panels.title: Interfaces Detail

panels.description: Count of interfaces currently in operational state UP. Pairs with Down — together they should equal the device's total interface count.
panels.title: Up

panels.description: Average inbound throughput across all interfaces, in bytes/s. Pairs with Outbound for a quick direction-shape read without scrolling to the timeseries.
panels.title: Inbound

panels.description: Per-interface traffic with the +inbound / -outbound mirror convention so you can read direction at a glance. Y-axis is bytes/s; absolute values are what matter.
panels.title: Traffic over time

panels.description: Average outbound throughput across all interfaces, in bytes/s.
panels.title: Outbound

panels.description: Number of routes the device's BGP peers have advertised that the device accepted into its RIB. Stable in normal operation; dips can mean a peer is flapping or filtering changed.
panels.title: Prefixes Accepted

panels.description: Per-peer BGP session state over the time range. Look for ESTABLISHED bands; transitions to IDLE or ACTIVE are session resets, which the `BgpSessionNotUp` alert rule fires on after 30s.
panels.title: BGP States

panels.description: All log lines emitted for `$device`, regardless of pipeline. Format: `[severity] message`. The pipeline label (direct vs vector) is visible if you expand a line — same data, different transport.
panels.title: Device Logs

title: Device Health
